Inclusion_criteria: CDI ≥10 and HRSD ≥10; grades 7–12; living at home with parent/guardian; no psychotic or suicidal symptoms; not in psychotherapy; reading level above a 6th-grade equivalence
Identified by community facilitators as exhibiting low mood, negative affect, depressive presentations, or significant levels of stress; sufficient English comprehension to use and understand SPARX.

Recruitment: Recruited from alternative education / alternative schooling programmes for adolescents excluded or alienated from mainstream education.

Inclusion_criteria: 12≤CESD-R≤40; no MDD/schizophrenia/bipolar;suicide-related hospitalization risk; no antidepressant or psychotropic medication use; no substance abuse; no reading impairment, no intellectual disability, visual impairment, or developmental disability

Recruitment: School+Community (School and juvenile justice/community agency recruitment)
Dosage: 30 sessions/6 weeks; encouraged to complete 1 mini-lesson per day; 1 module per week

Inclusion_criteria: No specific inclusion criteria regarding assessment;no depression severity threshold; fluent in English; written consent from gaurdians; have access for contact; ability of using smart phone
Funding: Philanthropic (Buxton Family Foundation); University (UNSW Brain Sciences)

Recruitment: Social media + Official website + Recommendation from others
Funding:

Inclusion_criteria: Girls had to score at or above the 70th percentile on depressive symptoms within the sample (RADS-2 score ≥ 59); girls with suicidal ideation or involving in ongoing psychotherapy were excluded; have a parent or guardian present who was capable of providing informed consent
Funding: Behavioural Science Institute, Radboud University Nijmegen

Inclusion_criteria:
The inclusion criteria for the study required participants to be English-speaking adolescents aged 13 to 17 who presented to the emergency department for any reason
; had to report experiencing physical peer violence (either as a victim or a perpetrator) within the past year and demonstrate mild-to-moderate depressive symptoms, defined as a Patient Health Questionnaire-9 (PHQ-9) score between 5 and 19
; participants were required to possess a mobile phone with text-messaging capabilities and have a parent or guardian present who was capable of providing informed consent.
Dosage: 1 brief in-ED intervention session + 8 weeks automated text messaging
Funding: National Institute of Mental Health grants K23 MH095866, K24 HD062645, and K24DA027109

Inclusion_criteria: CES-D-based 1-page screener: endorsed ≥2 items; current MDD & acute SI excluded; written consent from gaurdians and paricipants
Dosage: 6 weeks, self-paced (book); 2 reminder phone calls, only for pure encouragement

Inclusion_criteria: MFQ-C≥ 20, students who are having severe symptoms and/or significant risk requiring immediate intervention or having learning disability that might interfere with the intervention were exclued; writtenconsentfromgaurdiansandparicipants

CDRS-R≥30 or RADS-2≥76; self-referred for low mood; excluding students with high risk of suicidality; moderate-to-severe learning disorder; low ability of English and using computer; written form of consent by participants and their gaurdians
Dosage: 7 modules, ~25–30 min/module, 4–10 weeks; 1 weekly challenge at the end of each session

Recruitment: School (Mass mailings + handbills during lunch hour + posters in schools)

Inclusion_criteria: MFQ ≥ 20（validation research proposes ≥20 indicates any depressive disorder）; aged 12–18; referred for low mood/depression via primary mental health worker; excluding psychosis and active suicidality
